channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: TikTok says it will 'go dark' unless it gets clarity from Biden following Supreme Court ruling
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — TikTok said it will have to “go dark” this weekend unless the outgoing Biden administration assures the company it won’t enforce a shutdown of the popular app after the Supreme Court on Friday unanimously upheld the federal law banning the app unless it’s sold by its China-based pa
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Homes were burning and roads already jammed when Pacific Palisades evacuation order came, AP finds
item.description: The first evacuation order covering neighborhoods closest to the start of the devastating Pacific Palisades wildfire didn’t come until about 40 minutes after some of those homes were already burning, according to an Associated Press analysis of emergency communications and interviews with survivors.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, GENE JOHNSON, CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER, CLAUDIA LAUER and AMY TAXIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Kevin O'Leary says he's offering $20B cash for TikTok
item.description: Investor Kevin O’Leary, widely known as a star from “Shark Tank,” said he offered TikTok’s owners $20 billion in cash to buy the platform during a Friday appearance on Fox News’s “America’s Newsroom.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9

item.title: Justices agree to hear Maryland case on parents' rights and LGBTQ books
item.description: The parents, represented by the Becket Fund for Religious Liberty, sued Montgomery County Public Schools in 2023 after the school district said it would no longer honor opt-out requests or notify families if a book referencing gender or sexuality would be read in class.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, WGN 9

item.title: South Carolina man pleads guilty to the carjacking and killing of a New Mexico police officer
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — A South Carolina man pleaded guilty Friday to charges that he gunned down a New Mexico state police officer who had stopped to help him, marking a turning point in a case that had been set for trial later this year.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: California is years behind in implementing a law to make homes more fire resistant
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — Reeling from destructive wildfires, including the deadliest in California history, state lawmakers in 2020 passed new requirements for clearing combustible materials like dead plants and wooden furniture within 5 feet (1.5 meters) of homes in risky areas.
item.dc:creator: TRÂN NGUYỄN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sealy Mattress company is sued after Georgia man was found dead inside a locked trailer
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A Georgia man suffering a mental health crisis wandered into a Sealy Mattress facility, and his body was found locked inside the back of a truck there about a week later, his family says in a wrongful death lawsuit filed this week.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wisdom of a California grandmother, 90, goes viral amid Eaton Fire tragedy
item.description: A nearly 90-year-old California woman who lost her home to the Eaton Fire is going viral for pragmatism and positivity in the face of so much adversity.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KRON4

item.title: College students accused of online 'catch a predator' plot appear in court
item.description: Six Assumption University students were in court to face charges in a connection made between a Holyoke woman and a man on Tinder, which resulted in a TikTok plot to confront a falsely accused sexual predator.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Shook, KRON4

item.title: Bail denied for American captured in France and accused of assaulting Pennsylvania student
item.description: EAST BERLIN, Pa. (AP) — An American accused of sexually assaulting a college student in 2013 and later sending her a Facebook message that said “So I raped you” was ordered held without bail Friday during a court hearing in Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Driver in Texas migrant smuggling run that led to the deaths of 53 people pleads guilty
item.description: A Texas truck driver is facing jailtime after being charged with the deaths of 53 illegal aliens after transporting them in poor conditions resulting in death.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Q&A: 'God Bless the USA' singer Lee Greenwood on Donald Trump's inauguration and Bibles
item.description: Forty years ago, Lee Greenwood released “God Bless the U.S.A.,” a patriotic anthem that has become a standard at political gatherings. He's performed the song for a number of presidents, including Ronald Reagan and George H. W. Bush.
item.dc:creator: THE ASSOCIATED PRESS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: White House says TikTok ban will fall to Trump administration
item.description: The White House said Friday that the TikTok ban will fall to President-elect Trump's administration after the Supreme Court upheld a law requiring the app's China-based parent company to divest from it.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pepsi discontinues 'permanent' flavor, debuts a completely different one
item.description: Pepsi is planning to debut a brand-new flavor this month, marking its first “permanent” addition to the brand’s beverage portfolio in four years.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mel Gibson ‘just as surprised’ as anyone to be named Trump ‘special envoy’ to Hollywood
item.description: Actor and film producer Mel Gibson said he was stunned to be appointed a special envoy to Hollywood under the upcoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court gives green light to law that could ban TikTok
item.description: WASHINGTON — The Supreme Court on Friday delivered a blow to TikTok by upholding a law that could potentially lead to the video-sharing social media platform being banned in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Lawrence Hurley and Kat Tenbarge, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court upholds TikTok ban
item.description: The Supreme Court on Friday unanimously upheld a law requiring TikTok’s China-based parent company to divest from the app, teeing up a ban set to take effect Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, WGN 9

item.title: FDA will allow Zyn nicotine pouches to stay on the market
item.description: Zyn products will be allowed to stay on the market after federal health officials said Thursday that the popular nicotine pouches can help adult smokers cut back or switch completely.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, Fox 5

item.title: Biden commutes the sentences of nearly 2,500 non-violent drug offenders
item.description: Biden said he is proud of his record on clemency and will “continue to review additional commutations and pardons” before he leaves office on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WGN 9

item.title: Biden won't enforce TikTok ban, official says, leaving fate of app to Trump
item.description: President Joe Biden won't enforce a ban on the social media app TikTok that is set to take effect a day before he leaves office on Monday, a U.S. official said Thursday, leaving its fate in the hands of President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Mud, debris slide splits hillside home that survived Palisades Fire in half
item.description: A hillside home overlooking the Pacific Ocean that survived the devastating Palisades Fire has split in half from a landslide directly behind the property.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Diddy rape accuser tells her story in NewsNation interview
item.description: Ashley Parham sat down with NewsNation to discuss the harrowing allegations in a civil lawsuit she filed against Combs.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Banfield, NewsNation

item.title: Giuliani settles legal fight with former Georgia election workers and agrees to stop defaming them
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Rudy Giuliani reached a deal Thursday that lets the cash-strapped ex-New York City mayor keep his homes and belongings, including prized World Series rings, in exchange for unspecified compensation and a promise to never again speak ill of two former Georgia elections workers who won
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ash and other dangers mean LA area residents who fled fires a week ago won't be going home soon
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — It has been more than a week since two massive fires forced tens of thousands of people to flee their homes in the Los Angeles area, and officials said Thursday residents won't be going home soon.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. BLOOD and JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Where are they? Help sought finding those missing after Eaton fire in California
item.description: Anyone with information about the missing individuals was asked to contact the Sheriff's Department's Missing Persons Unit at 323-890-5500.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, NewsNation

item.title: Where are they? Help sought finding those missing after Eaton Fire in California
item.description: Authorities are asking the public for help in their search for several Altadena residents who remain unaccounted for after the deadly Eaton Fire burned thousands of homes.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KRON4

item.title: Police detain man dressed in fire gear in Pacific Palisades after reported looting
item.description: Editor’s Note: This article has been updated to include new information provided by the L.A. County Sheriff’s Office. LOS ANGELES (KTLA) – Two men, one of whom was dressed as a firefighter, were detained by L.A.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Live Updates: Trump nominees grilled by senators; Turner ouster draws bipartisan ire
item.description: Thursday marked another busy day of hearings for President-elect Trump's Cabinet nominees as his inauguration approaches.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, WGN 9

item.title: Biden warns of dangers of 'oligarchy' of ultra-rich, 'tech-industrial complex' running country
item.description: President Joe Biden will deliver his farewell address to the nation on Wednesday, days before President-elect Donald Trump returns to the White House.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Alabama inmate asks court to block nitrogen gas execution
item.description: MONTGOMERY, Ala. (AP) — Attorneys for an Alabama inmate scheduled to be the fourth person put to death with nitrogen gas on Wednesday asked a federal judge to block the execution, arguing that the first three inmates showed signs of suffering from suffocation as the gas flowed.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: For LA water issues, misinformation spreads nearly as fast as the wildfires
item.description: A billionaire couple was accused of withholding water that could help stop Los Angeles' massive wildfires. Democratic leadership was blamed for fire hydrants running dry and for an empty reservoir. Firefighters were criticized for allegedly using “women's handbags" to fight the fires.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN and BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pennsylvania grandfather in legal battle over backyard coaster
item.description: Some people build swing sets for their grandkids but Michael Chorba of Columbia County, Pennsylvania, has taken that a step further. He's refurbished an old rollercoaster on his property.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Sack, KRON4

item.title: 2 private lunar landers head toward the moon in a roundabout journey
item.description: CAPE CANAVERAL, Fla. (AP) — In a two-for-one moonshot, SpaceX launched a pair of lunar landers Wednesday for U.S. and Japanese companies looking to jumpstart business on Earth’s dusty sidekick.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Disabled Los Angeles fire victim escaped flames in wheelchair
item.description: Galen Buckwalter, who is paralyzed from the waist down, said he watched as the glow of the wildfire raced down the San Gabriel Mountains toward their home in the foothill community of Sierra Madre.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Actor Bill Hader 'in shock' returning to Palisades Fire neighborhood
item.description: "It's just gone. Everything," "Barry" actor Bill Hader said as he returned to the Pacific Palisades area.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, WGN 9

item.title: Officials tout Gaza ceasefire, hostage deal as Israel says details still in flux
item.description: Israel and Hamas agreed to pause the devastating war in the Gaza Strip, mediators announced Wednesday, raising the possibility of winding down the deadliest and most destructive fighting between the bitter enemies.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: US population projections shrink from last year because of declining birth rates, less immigration
item.description: The Congressional Budget Office has shrunk its projections for the U.S. population in 30 years to 372 million residents, a 2.8% drop from last year, citing declining birth rates and less expected immigration.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, ABC 4
